svg.aria-label: The forgetting lifecycle of a memory, in three stages. Stage one, WRITE: a new fact arrives carrying an importance weight, for example the user is Dana with importance 0.9. Stage two, MANAGE: at read time each fact is scored as importance times recency times access, and importance decays with age; a table shows scores falling from t equals 0 to t equals 6, the user is Dana from 0.90 to 0.38, the order from 0.70 to 0.25, prefers email from 0.60 to 0.21, raining today from 0.20 to 0.07. Stage three, FORGET: supersession retires an old fact when a newer one replaces it, prefers email is retired for prefers phone; and eviction drops the lowest-scoring fact when the store is over capacity 4, so raining today is evicted. Below, a consolidation arrow shows a sleep-time pass distilling four raw episodic events into one durable semantic fact, user name equals Dana, and one procedural rule, returns after the 30-day window apply a 10% restocking fee, after which the episodic log is pruned.
svg.title: The forgetting lifecycle: write, manage, forget, plus a sleep-time consolidation pass

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: A store that only grows fills with stale and duplicate facts. Score reads by importance x recency x access; let writes decay, supersede, and evict.
